title: Wig conspiracy behind Ketan's murder? Father says Siya Goyal’s family knew about his hair patch
content: The father of murdered Pune realtor Ketan Agarwal met the Maharashtra CM, demanding the death penalty for the accused. The CM assured a fast-tracked investigation and an SIT formation. Police revealed the victim was pushed from Lohagad Fort after the accused allegedly feared family disrepute if they eloped. Both suspects have confessed to the conspiracy and murder.

title: Ram Temple donation row: Rs 80 lakh recovered during probe; all 8 accused sent to 3-day judicial custody
content: Eight individuals arrested in connection with the alleged theft of Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Temple donations have been remanded to judicial custody until June 29. Investigators have recovered nearly Rs 80 lakh believed to be linked to the embezzled funds. Preliminary findings suggest six of the accused, employed by a bank's outsourced cash management agency, were directly involved in counting the donations when the alleged misappropriation occurred.

title: Has US-Iran deal erased Middle East pain for India inc?
content: Global market jitters over Middle East tensions have eased following a US-Iran ceasefire, improving the outlook for Indian corporate profitability. Crisil Ratings now anticipates a smaller 100-basis-point decline in operating margins for fiscal 2027, down from a previous 200-basis-point estimate. While most sectors are expected to see minimal disruption, some like airlines and specialty chemicals remain under pressure due to persistent input cost challenges.

title: 'Let's play like Lions': Shreyas Iyer's first message as India's new T20I captain
content: Shreyas Iyer is set to lead India's T20I side against Ireland, marking a significant return to the national setup with added captaincy responsibility.  Head coach Gautam Gambhir expressed confidence in Iyer's leadership, while the new skipper emphasized building a supportive and fun team culture. Iyer urged his teammates to play fearlessly and with belief, aiming to usher in a new era for the team.

title: Why India making its own bullet trains is a big deal - explained
content: The trains that will run on India’s first bullet train corridor will be manufactured indigenously. And that will be a feat for India’s engineering capabilities. A Rs 866.87 crore contract has been awarded to BEML Limited by Integral Coach Factory (ICF) Chennai for manufacturing two prototype bullet train or high-speed train sets.

title: After 'banning' Anthropic’s AI, US govt now sends message to OpenAI CEO Sam Altman
content: The US government has asked OpenAI to limit its new GPT-5.6 model to government-approved partners before any wider launch, citing security concerns. The move follows the administration forcing Anthropic to pull its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models offline weeks earlier. CEO Sam Altman called the staggered rollout not OpenAI's "preferred" approach, as Washington tightens oversight of powerful AI models under Trump's June executive order.

title: India reign supreme! Shooters top ISSF Jr World C'ship medal tally in Germany
content: Team India has triumphed at the ISSF Junior World Championship 2026, topping the medal standings with an impressive 24 medals, including seven golds. Young talents shone across rifle and pistol disciplines, securing victories in individual, mixed, and team events. This remarkable achievement underscores India's growing prowess in world shooting, with the NRAI crediting effective grassroots programs for the widespread success.

title: Ketan Agrawal murder case: Siya is ‘spinning stories’ during interrogation, say police
content: The Ketan Agarwal murder case has taken a new turn as Lonavala police on Friday said the prime accused, Siya, has given conflicting statements during interrogation, while chats recovered from mobile phones appear to contradict her claims.

title: 'Even vice-captains get dropped': Ex-India cricketer's blunt warning to Tilak Varma
content: Former India batter Mohammad Kaif cautioned young Tilak Varma, newly appointed T20I vice-captain, that the role offers no job security. Kaif highlighted recent instances like Axar Patel and Shubman Gill losing their positions despite leadership roles, emphasizing that consistent performance is paramount in the current Indian team.  He stressed that every player, regardless of designation, must continually prove their worth to retain their spot in the playing XI.

title: Bill Gates admits to the House panel that he had at least three extramarital affairs
content: Bill Gates revealed to Congress that Jeffrey Epstein attempted to leverage knowledge of Gates' extramarital affairs to re-establish contact after Gates sought to end their association. While no direct threats were made, Epstein allegedly used intermediaries for veiled warnings and explored blackmail tactics. Gates acknowledged past affairs, emphasizing Epstein's lack of involvement and that he only learned of them post-professional ties.

title: 'Commendable efforts': PM Modi's birthday wish to Pradhan gets community note on X
content: "Birthday wishes to Union Minister Shri Dharmendra Pradhan Ji. He is making commendable efforts towards the implementation of the National Education Policy, which seeks to make India a hub for knowledge, learning and innovation. Praying for his long and healthy life," Modi wrote on X. The post now carries a community note, which is a user-contributed fact-check or contextual note added to X posts.

title: Facebook’s ex-exec who claimed Sheryl Sandberg asked assistant to 'come to bed' sues Meta
content: Former Facebook executive Sarah Wynn-Williams is suing Meta, alleging the tech giant is using an invalid arbitration agreement to enforce a gag order, silencing her for over a year. Her upcoming memoir details controversial claims against top leadership, including Sheryl Sandberg and Joel Kaplan. Meta denies the allegations, calling the book false and defamatory, and states Wynn-Williams was fired for poor performance.

title: Paris Diamond League in doubt as police seek cancellation due to historic heat wave
content: A historic heatwave has put this weekend's Paris Diamond League athletics meet in doubt. French authorities have requested its cancellation, citing immense pressure on emergency services and extreme weather.  While organisers initially planned a modified event for elite athletes, the police prefecture has warned of an enforced cancellation if organisers don't comply. This unprecedented heat has already broken national records.

title: Kangana Ranaut reacts to Ketan Agarwal death, says parents can't be blamed
content: A Pune man's death, initially ruled an accident, is now a murder investigation. His fiancée and her alleged partner are accused of his killing. Amidst public discussion, Kangana Ranaut commented on parental responsibility, highlighting the significant impact of social media, AI, and peer groups on individuals' choices, suggesting families shouldn't be solely blamed for their children's actions.

title: Gurgaon cop removed from duty after video shows him abusing auto driver who refused free ride
content: A home guard with Gurgaon Police faced immediate removal from duty after a viral video captured him allegedly hurling abuse at an auto-rickshaw driver. The incident reportedly began when the driver refused a free ride, citing a prior app booking. The driver's account suggests the officer boarded his auto and demanded a free trip. Gurgaon Police confirmed the action, initiating departmental proceedings and vowing further steps based on inquiry findings.

title: DU UG admissions 2026: CSAS portal registration likely to open on June 27, here's how to apply
content: Delhi University's undergraduate admissions for the 2026-27 academic year are set to commence with the CSAS portal opening on June 27.  Admissions will exclusively rely on CUET UG 2026 scores, offering over 71,000 seats across various programs. Students should prepare their documents and monitor the official portal for application steps, which involve registration, preference filling, and seat allotment rounds. The academic session begins July 21.

title: Not Sachin, Kohli: Aussie legend says Sooryavanshi was 1st cricketer he asked for selfie
content: If selected, Sooryavanshi will be 15 years and 91 days old, younger than Sachin Tendulkar was when he made his international debut against Pakistan in 1989 at the age of 16 years and 205 days.

title: Op Amistad: India deploys two C17s to quake-hit Venezuela with medical supplies, aid
content: India has launched 'Operation Amistad' to aid earthquake-stricken Venezuela, dispatching two Indian Air Force (IAF) C-17 aircraft. These planes carry a 41-member Army field hospital unit and over 35 tonnes of vital relief supplies, including medicines and advanced medical equipment like BHISHM Cubes. This humanitarian mission underscores India's commitment to supporting Venezuela during its challenging post-earthquake recovery efforts.

title: For first time, names of soldiers martyred during Operation Sindoor made public
content: Six brave Indian soldiers, including five from the Army and one from the Air Force, who sacrificed their lives during Operation Sindoor following the Pahalgam terror attack, have been identified. Their names will be permanently inscribed at the National War Memorial. The operation saw precision strikes against terror launchpads, eliminating over 100 terrorists and showcasing India's resolute stance against extremism.

title: Under Gambhir's watchful eye, Sooryavanshi unleashes fiery shots - Watch
content: Fifteen-year-old batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has trained with the senior Indian cricket team ahead of their T20I series against Ireland. Spotted practicing attacking shots under coach Gautam Gambhir, Sooryavanshi could become India's youngest international debutant, potentially breaking Sachin Tendulkar's long-standing record. His inclusion presents a selection dilemma for the team management, balancing a winning combination with the excitement surrounding the young star.

title: Oldest-ever! Hugo Broos scripts WC history as SA reach knockouts for the first time
content: South Africa has made history by reaching the FIFA World Cup knockout stages for the first time, securing a crucial 1-0 win against South Korea. Coach Hugo Broos, at 74, became the oldest manager to win a World Cup match. Youngster Thapelo Maseko scored the decisive goal, showcasing the team's belief and tactical prowess. They now face Canada in the Round of 32.

title: Watch: Agra BJP corporator cuts birthday cake in drain to protest civic apathy
content: A BJP corporator in Agra staged a unique protest for his birthday, standing knee-deep in a filthy drain to highlight the municipal corporation's alleged neglect of drainage works.  Despite numerous complaints over three years, the drain remains unrepaired and uncleaned, impacting residents and leading to tragic incidents.  Local citizens are threatening further action if the issue isn't addressed.

title: Monsoon misfires: Over half of Telangana districts face rainfall stress, Kharif sowing at risk
content: Telangana faces significant rainfall stress, with 17 out of 33 districts experiencing deficient or largely deficient rainfall less than a month into the monsoon. This shortfall, particularly in key agricultural areas, raises concerns for kharif sowing. While the state's overall deficit is currently within normal limits, the uneven distribution and severe deficits in some districts highlight a growing regional imbalance, prompting calls for contingency plans.

title: Hybrid vs EV: Delhi GoM divided over subsidy plan in new mobility policy
content: Delhi's EV Policy 2.0 faces a hurdle as ministers debate incentives for strong hybrid vehicles. While some see them as a crucial stepping stone to cleaner transport, others insist on focusing solely on zero-emission electric cars. Hybrids, currently a significant part of Delhi's vehicle registrations, offer a compromise for those wary of EV range and cost, but experts argue for a complete shift to electric mobility to meet long-term goals.

title: CM Vijay runs 6 km marathon anti-drug event, videos praise his fitness
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay, at 53, demonstrated remarkable fitness by completing a 6km 'Start Run, Stop Drugs' marathon in Chennai. Leading thousands, including ministers and volunteers, he administered an anti-drug pledge and actively participated, even offering water to fellow runners. Videos of his energetic run and motivational presence have gone viral, highlighting his disciplined lifestyle and commitment to public awareness.

title: 'Challenging': Lalit Prabhakar on playing 26/11 terrorist Ajmal Kasab in Rajkummar Rao's 'Prahaar'
content: In 'Prahaar,' actor Lalit Prabhakar delves into the complex role of Ajmal Kasab, taking on a character that reexamines the events of the 26/11 Mumbai attacks. He consciously opts for unconventional roles to shift public perception and engage audiences in unique ways. Prabhakar reveals his admiration for the heroes involved in the tragedy, describing Kasab as a pivotal character that shapes his acting journey.

title: SpiceJet to pay Rs 62,000 after denying boarding to couple with valid passes
content: SpiceJet has been ordered by a Srinagar consumer commission to pay Rs 62,078 to a Haj couple denied boarding despite having valid passes. The airline, failing to appear, was found guilty of deficiency in service and unfair trade practice. The couple, returning from pilgrimage, faced a night at the airport and rebooked flights at a higher cost due to the airline's inexplicable refusal to let them board.

title: 'Influential people' spared: Shankaracharya targets Ram Temple probe, calls BJP's Hindutva 'fake'
content: Swami Avimukteshwaranand Saraswati has questioned the FIR in the Ram Temple donation embezzlement case, alleging it targets only lower-level employees while sparing those responsible for larger wrongdoing. He criticized the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust's functioning, claiming it disregarded religious traditions and was politically influenced. The seer also accused the BJP of having "fake" Hindutva, urging voters to support candidates committed to Sanatan Dharma.

title: Amid India-US trade deal talks, Trump admin’s fresh warning to world: ‘Old tariffs may return’
content: The latest statements by Bessent indicate that the Donald Trump administration is looking at alternative ways to impose tariffs on its trading partners since the US Supreme Court has ruled reciprocal tariffs as illegal.

title: The Siachen battle Pakistan thought it can't lose: At 21,500 feet, how India captured Bana Post
content: In 1987, India secured the Siachen heights, leading to a fierce battle for Quaid Post. Operation Rajiv saw Indian soldiers, under immense hardship and enemy fire, capture the strategically vital post. This victory, marked by extraordinary bravery and sacrifice, solidified India's control over the world's highest battlefield, a testament to the resilience of its soldiers against impossible odds.

title: Cash, gold, diamonds and flats: Suspended Shamirpet tahsildar under ACB scanner over Rs 20cr assets
content: Anti-Corruption Bureau sleuths unearthed assets worth an estimated Rs 20 crore during a fresh probe into a revenue official already arrested for bribery. Searches at four locations revealed properties, cash, jewellery, and bank deposits disproportionate to her known income. The tahsildar, T Sucharita, was previously arrested in May for demanding and accepting a bribe for an official favour and is currently in judicial remand.

title: 'Do not cast aspersions on Ayodhya': UP CM Adityanath on arrests in Ram temple donation theft case
content: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath vowed strict action against those found guilty of embezzling donations at the Ayodhya Ram Temple, following an FIR against eight individuals. He emphasized the government's swift response after an SIT report, urging against politicizing faith and asserting that evidence will be presented. The accused, involved in counting temple cash, have been arrested and are under questioning.

title: Nihang-police standoff at Uttarakhand border ends after talks, group returns to Himachal
content: Tension at the Himachal Pradesh-Uttarakhand border eased early Friday after a standoff involving Nihangs concluded peacefully. The group, which had clashed with police and breached barricades en route to Hemkund Sahib, agreed to return to Paonta Sahib following negotiations. This development resolved a protest demanding the release of four Nihangs arrested after a June 16 clash in Karnaprayag.

title: Passport citizenship row: Shashi Tharoor questions govt clarification, offers solution
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor has urged a legislative overhaul to make passports and Aadhaar cards conclusive proof of Indian citizenship. He highlighted the public confusion following the MEA's clarification that passports are travel documents, not definitive citizenship proof. Tharoor argued that this creates an "absurd legal paradox" for citizens and proposed a dual-document policy to resolve the issue.

title: Tiruvallur ammonia leak: Left parties seek ₹25-lakh compensation for families of victims
content: In a statement, CPI(M) Tamil Nadu Politburo member K. Balakrishnan alleged the management had housed young women workers from other States in hostel accommodation under exploitative conditions and made them work until midnight, leading to the incident

title: SIR completes one year: Nearly six crore names deleted so far
content: Post Bihar SIR, its voters' list was pruned by nearly 65 lakh amid allegations by the Opposition and activists that the poll authority was working at the behest of the BJP to disenfranchise citizens for want of documents

title: Sanjay Jaju appointed as next Telangana Chief Secretary
content: A 1992 batch IAS officer Sanjay Jaju has been preferred in spite of presence of two of his batchmates, Jayesh Ranjan and Vikas Raj, owing primarily to his stint in the central services since the formation of Telangana State without any connection whatsoever with the previous BRS government

title: Death toll in Kolkata warehouse collapse rises to 15
content: Rescue operations are being carried out for the third consecutive day at the site of the accident, by NDRF, Indian Army, Kolkata Police and other agencies

title: Government to amend NDPS Act to plug loopholes exploited by narco syndicates: Shah
content: Mr. Shah said that the value of the drugs seized between 2004 and 2014 was approximately ₹40,000 crore, whereas the value of the drugs seized between 2014 and 2026 is approximately ₹1.84 lakh crore

title: CPI(M) leader M.V. Jayarajan accuses Kerala’s UDF govt of curbing free speech through police and Meta
content: Jayarajan alleges that police cases are being registered against social media users and that Meta has removed posts critical of Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan under Information Technology Act section

title: Kolkata Cloud Chasers: The group that chases cyclones, thunderstorms and lightning across Bengal
content: For more than a decade, these Kolkatans have spent their weekends driving towards cyclones, thunderstorms and towering storm cells. Armed with weather models, radar imagery and cameras, the Kolkata Cloud Chasers pursue Bengal’s most dramatic weather events in search of photographs, data and a feeling they say cannot be bought.

title: Mirwaiz says Modi, among longest serving PMs of India, should opt for dialogue, diplomacy to resolve issues
content: “PM Modi is today among the longest-serving leaders in independent India. When he assumed office [in 2014], the PM spoke of regional cooperation and demonstrated an interest in improving relations with neighbouring countries. Those gestures generated hope across South Asia,” said the Mirwaiz

title: ‘It’s about time’: How a Chennai journalist’s book inspired ‘Made in India: A Titan Story’
content: Former The Hindu BusinessLine journalist Vinay Kamath reflects on seeing his book on Titan’s journey transformed into one of 2026’s streaming successes

title: Drug cases can cost government jobs and passports, warns Annamayya SP
content: The Superintendent of Police warned traders against selling nicotine products near schools, as drug-testing kits were supplied to every station in the district

title: Ram temple donation row: All eight accused named in FIR arrested
content: All the accused were in Ayodhya itself and were arrested late Thursday night. Further questioning is underway. Police are preparing to present them before a magistrate, says a senior official.

title: VB-G RAM G: Karnataka wants existing MGNREGA issues resolved before transition
content: The Karnataka government has requested the Centre to address the digital attendance-related issues faced under the existing scheme so that the same problems are not carried forward into the new framework.

title: Supreme Court lays to rest 30-year-old case of man’s death over a ₹500 watch
content: The top court Bench headed by Justice Ujjal Bhuyan closed criminal proceedings in the culpable homicide case after noting that two of the three accused persons have died during the pendency of the appeal

title: In Kalaburagi, a call for collective fight against drug abuse
content: From January 2025 till date, around 600 cases had been registered in Kalaburagi against both peddlers and consumers, according to the city police.

title: Why is Tamil Nadu opposing the Centre’s latest guidelines on Cauvery basin? | Explained
content: The fresh norms require the State government to prepare and submit a preliminary report, pre-feasibility report or concept note in tune with what has been stipulated in the 2017 guidelines and with the 2007 final award of the Tribunal

title: Commuter's metal chain disrupted Namma Metro service at Cubbon Park station, states BMRCL
content: The chain got ledged in the door guideway, resulting in the door not closing and the train being unable to leave the station

title: Amazon eyes on West Bengal's 70,000 sellers as Prime Day sale returns
content: In Bengal, Amazon’s network includes 03 Fulfilment Centres and 03 Sortation Centres, more than 1050 Amazon Hub Delivery and almost 100 Amazon-owned and Partner Delivery Stations across the state

title: Some Congress leaders signal possible rethink on Bidadi Township Project if farmers are unwilling to part with lands
content: The cautious position adopted by Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee (KPCC) president B.K. Hariprasad and Home Minister Priyank Kharge on the proposed acquisition of agricultural land for the Bidadi Township Project, coupled with suggestions that the project may be shelved if farmers refuse to part with their holdings, is expected to strengthen the resolve of protesting farmers and provide fresh ammunition to the BJP-JD(S) alliance

title: Those who target schools, children with impunity must be held into account: India tells UNSC
content: The United Nations verified 38,558 grave violations, which affected 24,174 children in 2025 (15,493 boys, 7,990 girls, 691 sex unknown), the highest number of children affected by grave violations since the beginning of the mandate

title: We will send resignation letter as birthday gift, please resign: CJP founder Dipke to Pradhan
content: “We will send a resignation letter as a birthday gift, please just stamp it with your thumb,” says Abhijeet Dipke

title: Bengaluru triple murder accused Kenneth taken into custody in Puducherry
content: A 55-year-old man, his wife, and their younger daughter were murdered in an apartment at Seegehalli, under K.R. Puram Police Station limits in east Bengaluru, on June 22 night

title: Andhra Pradesh plans re-auction of Ramagiri gold blocks in Ananthapuramu
content: The two blocks in Ramagiri mandal, last mined under John Taylor and Sons a century ago, cover 2,000 hectares and will be offered under a Composite Licence

title: The debate over the proposed NFSA amendment | Explained
content: The Centre has proposed revising the foodgrain entitlement for AAY beneficiaries under the NFSA. While it says the change will ensure fairer distribution, critics fear it could reduce allocations for many households

title: Madras High Court sees unusual surge in election petitions after 2026 Tamil Nadu, Puducherry polls
content: Around 55 election petitions, challenging the victory of several high-profile candidates, have been filed in 2026 with respect to both Tamil Nadu and Puducherry Assembly elections, as against 23 petitions filed in 2016 and just 14 petitions in 2021

title: UCC to be implemented in Bengal by following all procedures: Suvendu
content: The development could mark one of the most consequential legal and social policy shifts in Bengal in recent times, with the BJP holding the UCC as representing both a major governance promise and a politically significant reform

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay flags off anti-drug marathon, runs to spread awareness
content: The Chief Minister signed on the commitment board saying ‘Sports-ah edu, drugs-ah vidu’ (Take up sports, give up drugs).

title: Explained | Netra AEW&C: India’s indigenous ‘eye in the sky’ that strengthens air power
content: India is the fifth country in the world to develop this capability, as the Netra can detect, track, identify and monitor airborne and maritime targets enhancing the network-centric operations capabilities of the IAF

title: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann’s fake video controversy | Explained
content: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann has been embroiled in a controversy over a fake video. The Hindu explains the reason for the controversy, a new twist, the Chief Minister’s defence, and the road ahead for the AAP ahead of the 2027 Assembly election

title: Congress demands Supreme Court-monitored probe into alleged Ram Temple donation embezzlement case
content: Exploiting Lord Ram for votes and notes is unacceptable, the Congress party seeks the disbanding of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust.

title: Nihang-police standoff in Uttarakhand ends after talks, group returns to Himachal
content: The protesters declared they would not return to Punjab until four Nihang members arrested in connection with the Karnprayag incident were released

title: Telangana gets relief from over 42°C, highest temperature drops to 39°C on June 25
content: Heavy rainfall is very likely at isolated places in 14 Telangana districts on Saturday (June 27, 2026)

title: How this run club in Visakhapatnam is inspiring women to take up running and strength training
content: Strong Sisters Run Club is helping women in Visakhapatnam embrace running and strength training in a supportive, judgement-free space

title: Attempt to shield big people; junior employees being targeted: Kejriwal on Ram temple donation case
content: I prayed that whoever is guilty of this 'maha paap' (grave sin) should receive the strictest possible punishment from God, says AAP leader Arvind Kejriwal

title: Chennai’s Dr Priya Selvaraj on surviving a crevasse fall and summiting Mount Everest
content: Dr Priya Selvaraj trained for four months to summit Mount Everest. The daunting climb there and back, scaling the highest mountain in the world at 8,848.86 metres, taught her many lessons and even had her fighting for her life. Back in Chennai, this granddaughter of legendary actor Gemini Ganesan, recounts the gritty details of her journey

title: Four from Markapuram killed, six injured as SUV hits parked lorry on Macherla-Veldurthi highway
content: The SUV, carrying members of a family from Markapuram to Pamuru, hit a lorry parked after a tyre burst on the Macherla-Veldurthi highway in Palnadu

title: Light to moderate rainfall, thunderstorms recorded at isolated places in Rajasthan
content: According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), Jaipur, Aklera in Jhalawar district recorded the highest rainfall in the state at 51 mm

title: CBSE’s move to make vocational education mandatory for class IX sparks concern among Bengaluru schools
content: CBSE, under its NCF-SE, has introduced Kaushal Vikas (Vocational education) as a mandatory subject for class IX students, rather than keeping it optional

title: Man arrested for ‘vandalism’ at Hibi Eden MP’s residence
content: According to FIR, the accused committed the alleged offences as Hibi Eden had questioned certain communal remarks made by the accused during telephonic conversations between the two

title: AGICL invites bids for consultant to oversee ₹4,933-crore Amaravati Secretariat works
content: The consultant will supervise the MEP, facade, interiors and allied works of the 145-department complex, with bids to be opened on June 30

title: Mayor among BJP, CPI(M) councillors booked after clash at Thiruvananthapuram Corporation
content: Museum police register cases under various provisions of BNS, including those relating to unlawful assembly, rioting, voluntarily causing hurt, intentional insult and wrongful restraint

title: Rise in shrimp feed prices leaves aqua farmers in deep waters in Andhra Pradesh
content: For aqua farmers, the question is no longer whether shrimp are growing, but whether farming can remain profitable following a recent increase in the price of Vannamei shrimp feed by ₹10 per kg and Black Tiger shrimp feed by ₹12 per kg

title: Six more suspected Bangladeshi nationals arrested for staying illegally in Kochi
content: Police sources said the identity cards and other documents recovered from the arrested individuals were found to be forged

title: Monsoon deficit and India’s agricultural vulnerability: In graphics
content: With the monsoon’s deficit pegged at 43% for June, state-specific and crop-specific vulnerabilities remain for the year’s kharif season.

title: Anitha stresses need to protect youth from drug menace, pats police for 'zero ganja cultivation’
content: At an event marking International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking, she says drug abuse is not merely a social issue but a serious threat to the nation’s future, as it destroys the talent, health and aspirations of young people

title: Minister B.C. Janardhan Reddy tours Banaganapalle, reviews roads, drainage and schools
content: On a tour of his home constituency, the Minister inspected drainage and college works and told officials to fix sanitation problems in Santha Peta

title: T.N. Water Resources Department to move groundwater extraction NOC process online
content: Officials of the WRD said work is in progress to issue fresh NOCs through the online portal in about a month

title: Assam government relentlessly cracking down on drugs: Himanta
content: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma said the government was working to safeguard the future of the youth and save them from the menace of drug abuse

title: India’s ‘eye in the sky’ Netra AEW&C system gets Final Operational Clearance
content: Indigenous system has already proved its worth during Balakot strikes and Operation Sindoor;   scientists and IAF officers dedicate Netra clearance to colleagues killed in 1999 tragic air crash; ‘today, we have shown that their sacrifice has not gone in vain’, says DRDO Aeronautics Cluster chief

title: SIR: BJP, RSS conspiracy should not be allowed to succeed, says B.K. Hariprasad
content: KPCC President B.K. Hariprasad has said that while Congress is not opposed to Special Intensive Revision (SIR), the party is against the conspiracy hatched by RSS and BJP to remove names of genuine voters

title: Development of villages is key to building a prosperous Punjab: Bhagwant Singh Mann
content: For first time in India, Sarpanches will receive such significant honorarium; Congratulations to CM Bhagwant Singh Mann for this historic decision: Arvind Kejriwal

title: HMDA plans multi-level underground parking facility at Paigah Palace complex in Hyderabad
content: The palace is a Schedule IIB listed heritage site according to the HMDA’s own rules that require intelligent conservation

title: No Home, No Safety: How Exposed Are Children Growing Up On India's Streets
content: A 10-year-old girl who lived with her family on a Delhi footpath was allegedly kidnapped, raped and murdered on Monday, bringing renewed attention to the dangers faced by children growing up on the streets.

title: UP Man Leans Over To Get Ice Cream From Vendor, Falls From Balcony
content: The incident took place at the Hindalco Colony in Renukoot, with the man trying to purchase the ice cream from the first-floor balcony of the house.

title: Taiwan Ship Hit By Unknown Object Near Oman Before Crossing Hormuz Strait
content: After an initial inspection by the crew, damage was found around the bridge windows. But the crew, vessel and cargo are all safe.

title: After Sex, Drugs, Alcohol Rehab, Now There's De-Addiction For AI
content: AI addiction and dependence is reportedly on the rise. There are now support groups similar to Alcoholics Anonymous that can help those in need recover.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Pune Fort Murder, And The One Word That Could've Stopped It All
content: The gruesome killing of Ketan Agarwal and the death of Twisha Sharma last month raise questions about the rigid culture surrounding marriages in India

title: Rs 18-Crore Cannabis, Long Chase: 'Wholesaler' Couple Arrested In Kerala
content: Police had been tracking Ayush for several days. He was intercepted while riding a two-wheeler, where officers recovered nearly 1.5 kg of hybrid marijuana concealed in a bag.

title: Chhattisgarh Takes Big Step Towards Uniform Code, Forms Panel To Draft Bill
content: The panel, headed by former Supreme Court judge Justice Ranjana Prakash Desai, comprises Shatrughna Singh, MK Raut, Mohan Pawar and Jyoti Rani Singh as members.

title: Allegations To Arrest, Resignations: How Ram Mandir Donation Row Unravelled
content: Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust general secretary Champat Rai has now stepped down on moral grounds, and trustee Anil Mishra has also submitted his resignation.

title: 26/11 Prosecutor Ujjwal Nikam To Fight Pune Murder Victim's Case
content: In a post on X, Chief Minister's office, said Ketan Agarwal's father, Vishal Agarwal, met Devendra Fadnavis in Pune today and demanded justice for his son.

title: Blood 'Tilak', Pink Turban: 13-Year-Old Girl Makes History In Rajput Legacy
content: The custom of the "Paag Ka Dastoor" dates back hundreds of years, but for the first time at its centre was a young girl, Tejaswi Kumari Jodha, who was recognised as the successor to her father.

title: "Guilty Won't Be Spared": Yogi Adityanath On Ram Temple Donation Row
content: Yogi Adityanath described the controversy as an attempt to target Ram devotees and defame Ayodhya out of "frustration" by some political parties.

title: Ram Temple Trust Key Official Champat Rai Steps Down Amid Donation Row: Sources
content: Champat Rai was the general secretary of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, an independent body that manages the shrine.

title: "Operation Amistad Underway": India Dispatches Relief, Medical Aid To Earthquake-Hit Venezuela
content: The death count in Venezuela following the deadly double earthquakes has risen to 235, while 4,300 people have been injured.

title: Venezuela Earthquakes Live: 235 Killed, Over 1,500 Injured In Venezuela Earthquakes, Rescue Ops On
content: Venezuela earthquake LIVE Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.

title: An Organic Shift: Clean Eating, Healthy Snacking And A $10 Billion Market
content: One challenge continues to loom large: affordability.Most Indian consumers support the idea of organic food. Fewer actually buy it regularly.

title: Anti-Terror Agency NIA Files Chargesheet Against 11 Bangladeshi Terrorists
content: The conspiracy involved promotion of the terrorist organisation's extremist agenda, radicalisation of vulnerable youth, and expansion of the outfit's network in India.

title: Collapsed Buildings, Debris: Satellite Pics Show Impact Of Venezuela Earthquakes
content: High-resolution satellite imagery sourced from Vantor confirms the widespread structural damage caused by two deadly earthquakes in Venezuela.

title: Ram Temple Donation Row Live Updates: 'No One Will Be Spared': Yogi Adityanath On Donation Fraud
content: Ram Mandir Donation Row Live Updates: Eight persons named in an FIR over the alleged embezzlement of donations received at the Ram temple in Ayodhya have been arrested
